frontmatter.description: When the response cache misbehaves, restart the worker before reaching for a deeper invalidation strategy — but only for short-lived caches.

asset.body:
Cache invalidation heuristic

When the response cache returns stale data, the first step is usually to restart the worker process — most of our caches are in-process and short-lived.

If the restart does not clear the issue within one minute, escalate to a deliberate invalidation. The two-tier eviction tooling lives in `tools/cache-evict.ts`.

Note: this guidance applies to the request-path cache only. The build-artifact cache uses a different invalidation contract; see ops runbook 042 before touching it.

feedback.reason: Restart fixed it in under 30 seconds on May 1
Restart did not fix the build-artifact cache miss on May 6
feedback.note: Memory steered us toward a worker restart when the actual issue was in the artifact tier — guidance is too narrow.

feedback.reason: Ambiguous "short-lived" qualifier led to a wrong call during the May 15 incident

humanGrade.rationale: Mixed signal — one positive, two related negatives that surface a real-but-narrow scoping issue. MT-Bench-style judges should land this in the uncertainty band (2.5–3.5) and request human review per D-5 / #388, rather than auto-promote or auto-reject.
